document.title: Appendix 2. SDS (Software Design Specification) & Testing & Traceability
document.documentType: Software Design Specification, Testing and Traceability appendix
authorityOrJurisdiction: FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR Part 820
FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR Part 803
standards: FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 820
FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 803
CDRH Guidance: General Principles of Software Validation
CDRH Guidance: Off-The-Shelf Software Use in Medical Devices
CDRH Guidance: Guidance for the Submission of Premarket Notifications for Medical Image Management Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: AspenView revision 2 appendix describing software design specification, testing, verification, validation, and traceability.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports software validation documentation by relating SRS items to SDS design items, testing, hazard analysis, and referenced regulatory standards/guidance.
keyPoints: The document states its purpose is to describe relations between SRS, source code and the Operation Manual for traceability.
Software development follows a Waterfall Life Cycle Model with analysis, planning, design, implementation, qualification, and maintenance phases.
Testing sections cover code review, module review, integration module review, dynamic test, functional test, and traceability analysis.
The reference section lists EN 62304, EN ISO 14971, EN ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR Parts 820 and 803, CDRH software guidance, and IEEE software documentation standards.

evidence.claim: The document is AspenView Appendix 2 for SDS, testing, and traceability.
evidence.evidenceText: Appendix 2. SDS (Software Design Specification) & Testing & Traceability

evidence.claim: The document number is shown as AI-AspenView-SVR-003 in the opening pages.
The document is revision 2 with initiate date 2022.09.06.
Revision status lists revision number 2 dated 2020.08.01 with description update for risk management.
The document purpose is traceability among SRS, source code, and Operation Manual.
evidence.evidenceText: Purpose of this document is to describe relations between SRS, source code and Operation Manual. This document enumerates (enlists, formalizes) items of SRS and Operation Manual for traceability.

evidence.claim: The software development process follows a Waterfall Life Cycle Model.
evidence.evidenceText: Software development process follows classic methodology of Waterfall Life Cycle Model

evidence.claim: Qualification verifies the design and code meet original requirements, and testing checks intended outputs from defined inputs.
evidence.evidenceText: The qualification phase verifies that the design and code implemented meet the original requirements. Testing ensures that defined inputs will produce intended results that coincide with required outputs.

evidence.claim: Maintenance permits only defect fixes or approved enhancements after software release.
evidence.evidenceText: Once the software is released, the only software changes allowed are to fix Identified defects in the code or to support approved enhancements.

evidence.claim: SDS identifiers are grouped by common, acquisition, image processing, EUA, and check/logging/error handling features.
evidence.evidenceText: AIV-SDS-100..199 – Common features; AIV-SDS-200..299 – Acquisition features; AIV-SDS-300..399 – Image Processing features; AIV-SDS-400..499 – EUA; AIV-SDS-500..599 – Check, logging and error handling features

evidence.claim: Referenced standards and guidance include EN 62304, EN ISO 14971, EN ISO 13485, FDA 21 CFR Parts 820 and 803, CDRH guidance, and IEEE standards.
evidence.evidenceText: 6.2 Reference • EN 62304:2006/AC:2008 ... • EN ISO 14971:2012 ... • EN ISO 13485:2016/AC:2016 ... • FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 820 • FDA Quality System Regulation 21 CFR, Part 803 • CDRH Guidance: General Principles of Software Validation ... • IEEE 1012-1986 ... • IEEE 829-1983 ... • IEEE 1016 ... • IEEE 830

evidence.claim: Testing organization roles include SW Engineer, Team Manager, and Test Engineer.
evidence.evidenceText: Human Resource (Roles and Responsibilities) Person Responsibilities ... SW Engineer (3 person) ... Team Manager (1 person) ... Test Engineer (1 person) Software test

evidence.claim: The testing environment lists C# with Visual Studio 2022 and Roslyn.
evidence.evidenceText: Program Language : C# ... Editor : Visual Studio 2022 ... Compiler : Roslyn ( Visual Studio 2022) ... Debugger : Visual Studio 2022

evidence.claim: The dynamic test section records pass results for software run, login check, disk space, LAN port, detector status, Windows versions, image processing time, image quality, and PACS sending.
evidence.evidenceText: 6.4.4 Dynamic Test ... Pass(P) / Fail(F) ... A Run the software ... P ... I Send Image to PACS Server ... P

evidence.claim: The functional test section records pass results for installation, patient registration, image acquisition, image processing, DICOM transfer, export, deletion, e-labeling, printing, search, storage, PACS queue, application functions, stitching, and DICOM print.
evidence.evidenceText: 6.4.5 Functional Test ... A Installation ... P ... W Check, Application functions -ETC(Stitching, DICOM Print) P

evidence.claim: Traceability analysis maps SRS numbers to SDS numbers, hazard analysis identifiers, and test IDs.
evidence.evidenceText: 7. Traceability Analysis ... SRS SDS Hazard Analysis Number Test ID

evidence.claim: The source contains a document number inconsistency in later pages, changing from AI-AspenView-SVR-003 to RS-AspenView-SVR-003.

reviewFlags.reason: Document number conflicts within the source: early pages show AI-AspenView-SVR-003, while pages 32-46 show RS-AspenView-SVR-003.
Authority signal is FDA, but the text appears to be an internal AspenView validation/design document that references FDA/CDRH materials rather than an FDA-issued document.
Prepared by, reviewed by, approved by, inspected by, and reviewed by signature fields are present but not populated in the extracted text.
Some OCR/text quality issues are present, including split words and typographical errors such as Traceabiltiy, Libray, and phyisican.
